summary: Moderator application for Scrambly platform. Admin is Jourdain Rodriguez who can ban moderators. Moderators can warn users (unlimited warnings) and ban users for disrespectful, unkind, or inappropriate/pornographic content. Pornographic content results in instant ban until appeal. Users who receive an unfair warning can report the moderator to the admin, who can then ban that moderator.
architectural_notes: Admin account is fixed to Jourdain Rodriguez with elevated privileges over moderators
Pornographic content violations trigger automatic/instant ban pending appeal
imageRequirements:
## Required Images
- Dark shield badge logo with a stylized 'S' monogram, authority-themed moderation emblem (scrambly-mod-logo.dim_256x256.png)

existing_features.title: Build an admin panel page (accessible only to Jourdain Rodriguez) that shows: (1) a list of all moderators with their status and a 'Ban Moderator' action button for active moderators; (2) a list of all pending moderator reports with reporter info, reason, and 'Ban Moderator' / 'Dismiss' action buttons; (3) a list of all pending ban appeals with user info, appeal text, and 'Approve Appeal' / 'Deny Appeal' action buttons.

Build a moderator dashboard page (accessible only to active, non-banned moderators) that shows: (1) a searchable list of all users with their current status (active/warned/banned) and warning count; (2) per-user action buttons: 'Issue Warning' (with a reason input), 'Ban User' (with a reason input), and 'Instant Ban for Pornographic Content' (one-click, no extra input); (3) a disabled/greyed-out state for all action buttons if the moderator is banned.

Build a user-facing status page where a user can: (1) see if they are banned and the reason; (2) submit a ban appeal with a text message if no appeal is currently pending; (3) view their current appeal status (none / pending / approved / denied); (4) view their list of received warnings with reasons and timestamps; (5) report a moderator (with a reason text field) if they have received at least one warning — this submits a moderator report to the admin.

Apply a bold, dark-themed visual design across the entire app: deep charcoal/near-black backgrounds, high-contrast accent color (e.g., red-orange for destructive actions, teal/green for approvals), clean sans-serif typography, card-based layout for lists and panels, and clear visual hierarchy that communicates authority and safety for a moderation context.

new_feature_requests.summary: Moderator dashboard with user list and moderation actions (warn, ban)
Unlimited warnings system: moderators can issue warnings to users
User ban system: moderators can ban users for disrespectful/inappropriate behavior; pornographic content triggers instant ban
Ban appeal system: banned users can submit an appeal
User report-a-moderator feature: users who receive an unfair warning can report the moderator to the admin
Admin panel for Jourdain Rodriguez: admin can ban moderators based on user reports or other reasons

Build a backend data model and actor with the following entities: User (id, username, status: active/warned/banned, warningCount, banReason, banAppealText, banAppealStatus: none/pending/reviewed, isBanned), Moderator (id, principal, name, status: active/banned, banReason), Warning (id, targetUserId, issuedByModeratorId, reason, timestamp), ModeratorReport (id, reportedModeratorId, reportedByUserId, reason, timestamp, status: pending/resolved), and a hardcoded admin identity for 'Jourdain Rodriguez'. Persist all data in stable storage.

Implement backend functions for moderator actions: issueWarning(userId, reason) — add a warning to a user with no cap on how many warnings can be given; banUser(userId, reason) — ban a user for disrespectful, unkind, or inappropriate behavior; instantBanUser(userId) — immediately ban a user flagged for pornographic content (ban takes effect instantly, no confirmation step); all three functions must be callable only by authenticated moderators (not banned moderators).

Implement backend functions for the ban appeal system: submitAppeal(userId, appealText) — lets a banned user submit an appeal message; reviewAppeal(userId, decision: approve/deny) — admin-only function to approve (unban) or deny the appeal; getMyAppealStatus(userId) — returns current appeal status for a user.

Implement backend functions for the report-a-moderator system: reportModerator(moderatorId, reason) — lets a user who received at least one warning report the moderator who issued it; getModeratorReports() — admin-only query to list all pending reports; resolveModeratorReport(reportId, banModerator: Bool) — admin-only function to mark the report resolved and optionally ban the reported moderator.
